values.title: Tracking the Migrants onboard SS Devonshire from Ceylon to Queensland

entries.title: Landamullagay Barmis Singho
entries.description:
Landamullagay Barmis Singho

Sinhalese (possible): ලන්ද මුල්ලගේ බාමිස් සිඤ්ඤෝ

Born: 1871,  Bundragoda (Banduragoda), Ceylon

Died: 1934,  Childers, Bundaberg, Australia

 

Description:

Landamullagay Barmis Singho was born in 1871 to farmer Goda Landa Mullagay Romial Singho and Noi Harney. He migrated to Queensland aged 11 onboard the SS Devonshire and disembarked in Bundaberg. He married Lena Poddy Nona Semon in 1900 and had 13 children. From electoral rolls and city directories, it appears that Singho resided in Booyal most of his life and worked as a cane farmer. He died of heart failure aged 63 years in Childers, Bundaberg. At the time of his death, his occupation was listed as green grocer.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

Original data: Find a Grave. Find a Grave. http://www.findagrave.com/cgi-bin/fg.cgi.

 

2. Ancestry.com. Australia, Electoral Rolls, 1903-1980 [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2010.

Original data: Australian Electoral Commission. [Electoral roll].

 

3. Ancestry.com. Australia, City Directories, 1845-1948 [database on-line]. Provo, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2015.

Original data: Various publishers. Australian City Directories. Gould Genealogy & History, South Australia, Australia.

entries.title: Kallu (Call) Appoo
entries.description:
Kallu (Call) Appoo

Sinhalese: කලු අප්පු

 

Born: 1860,  Point de Galle, Ceylon

Died: 1954,  Mount Perry, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Kallu (Call) Appoo was born in 1860 to Mattis Appo and Eliza in Point de Galle, Ceylon. He came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 and disembarked in Bundaberg. He was one of the first settlers in the Goodnight Scrub and resided there for 60 years. Appoo married Sara Wanetonge in 1883 and had 10 children and was described as 'held in high esteem by settlers in the district'. He died in 1954 aged 94 in Gin Gin Hospital. He is buried in Goodnight Scrub cemetery.

 

Additional images:

 

Links:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia, Death Index, 1787-1985 [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2010.

2. https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Appoo-23

3. "Bundaberg in Print 110 years of News" (2009) and the Publisher : Bundaberg Newspaper Company, 22-24 Targo St, Bundaberg, QLD 4670

4. Ancestry.com. Australia, Marriage Index, 1788-1950 [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2010.

entries.title: Widdarulargay David Silva
entries.description:
Widdarulargay (David) Silva

 

Born: 1847,  Colombo, Ceylon

Died: 1923,  Sydney, New South Wales, Australia

 

Description:

Widdarulargay David Silva was born in 1847 in Colombo, Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 with his wife Mary Silva (nee Harmy) and two year-old daughter Celestina.  His family disembarked in Mackay and worked at Palms Plantation initially before moving to New South Wales. Records show that in 1891, Silva was charged with 'maliciously wounding with intent to do grievous bodily harm' in Grafton, New South Wales. His occupation there is listed as farmer. Silva died in 1923 in Murwillumbah and is buried in Tweed Heads, New South Wales.

 

Records:

NSW Archives (link unavailable)

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. New South Wales, Australia, Gaol Description and Entrance Books, 1818-1930 [database on-line]. Provo, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

2. https://thuppahis.com/2019/11/18/the-devonshire-reaches-queensland-with-500-cingalese-coolies-in-1882/

3. State Records Authority of New South Wales; Kingswood, New South Wales, Australia; Supreme Court: registers of criminal indictments 1863-1882 [frame no’s 7–307], 1882–93 [frame no’s 308-635], 1893–98 [frame no’s 636–925]; Series Number: NRS 13492; Reel: 1860

entries.title: James Gurusinghe Williams
entries.description:
James Gurusinghe Williams

Sinhalese: ජේමිස් ගුරුසිංහ විලිඅම්ස් 

 

Born: 1866,  Point de Galle, Ceylon

Died: 1954,  Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

James Gurusinghe Williams was born in Galle in 1866 and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 as a teenager. He settled in Mackay prior to relocating to Duranbah in New South Wales, with his wife Celestina, where they purchased land. In 1912, he was charged with assault in Grafton and his occupation is listed as a labourer. Williams died in 1954 in Queensland.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Public Member Trees [database on-line]. Provo, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2006.Original data: Family trees submitted by Ancestry members.

2. Ancestry.com. Australia, Marriage Index, 1788-1950 [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2010.

3. Ancestry.com. New South Wales, Australia, Gaol Description and Entrance Books, 1818-1930 [database on-line]. Provo, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

entries.title: Hendrick Appo
entries.description:
Hendrick Appoo

Sinhalese: හේන්ද්‍රික් අප්පු 

 

Born: 1861 or 1867, Ceylon

Died: 1936,  Mackay, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Hendrick Appoo was born in either 1861 or 1867 in Ceylon, and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882, and disembarked in Mackay, where he worked at the Homebush sugar plantation. He died in 1936 in Mackay.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

Bundaberg in print 110 years of news (2009)

​

entries.title: Sara Wenitong
entries.description:
Sara Wenitong

Sinhalese: සාරා වනිගතුංග 

 

Born: 1870,  Kandy, Ceylon

Died: 1937,  Maryborough, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Sara Wenitong was born in 1870 and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 with her brother David Weniton and her parents Harmanis and Lucy Anna Wanetonge. Sara married twice, both times to Devonshire migrants. Her first marriage was to Kallu Appo at age 13 in 1883, and to Amaris Appo in 1909.  She died in 1937.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave​

entries.title: Allis Appoo
entries.description:
Allis Appoo

Sinhalese: අල්විස් අප්පු

 

Born: Unknown, Ceylon

Died: 1914,  Gin Gin, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Allis Appoo was born in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882, and disembarked in Bundaberg. He worked in the Goodnight Scrub and was one of many Ceylonese carving a home there until he was killed in an accident involving a falling tree in 1914.

 

Newspaper Article:

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave (unavailable)

 

Sources:

1. https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/215704396?searchTerm=Allis%20Appo#

​

entries.title: John Silva
entries.description:
John Silva

Sinhalese (possible): ජෝන් සිල්වා

 

Born: 1865, Ceylon (estimated)

Died: Unknown, Ceylon

 

Description:

From his Certificate of Exemption record in 1914, John Silva was born around 1865 in Matara, Sri Lanka (written as Martora in the record). He was one of 500 who departed Galle, Ceylon for Bundaberg, Queensland in 1882 at the age of around 16 or 17. After arriving in Queensland, he worked as a cane farmer in Booyal. According to the below article, he is one of a few Devonshire migrants who saved up to return to Ceylon in his later years and presumably left Queensland permanently and died there.

 

No Records.

 

Sources:

1. https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/171884707#​

entries.title: Louis Appo
entries.description:
Louis Appoo

Sinhalese: ලුවිස් අප්පු 

 

Born: 1851 or 1852,  Ceylon

Died: 1937,  Mackay, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Louis Appoo was born in either 1851 or 1852 in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He disembarked in Mackay. It is ambiguous if he is the same Louis Appo mentioned in the local Mackay newspaper in 1884 that was charged with absconding service. He went missing in 1937 after going to bathe at the local creek, and shortly after was found to have died from natural causes.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/199397464/louis-appo

2. https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/170264398?searchTerm=Appo%20Cingalese

3. https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/169530030?searchTerm=Louis%20Appo%20Mackay

entries.title: Peter Appoo
entries.description:
Peter Appoo

Sinhalese: පීටර් අප්පු  

 

Born: Unknown,  Ceylon

Died: 1916,  Gin Gin, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Peter Appoo was born in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire to Bundaberg. He worked at the Goodnight Scrub with other Ceylonese workers, until he died of burns in a work-related accident in 1916.

 

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

entries.title: Henry Appoe
entries.description:
Henry Appoe

Sinhalese: හෙන්රි අප්පු 

 

Born: 1845,  Ceylon

Died: 1924,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Henry Appoe was born in 1845 in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He lived in Bundaberg, Maryborough and Gympie working as a cook, until he was admitted to the Dunwich Lazarat in 1909. He was discharged in 1912, and died in 1924 from senility.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/160512161/henry-appoe​

entries.title: Aposenio
entries.description:
Aposenio

Sinhalese (possible): අප්පු සිඤ්ඤෝ

 

Born: 1863,  Colombo, Ceylon

Died: 1913,  Peel Island, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Aposenio was born in 1863 in Colombo and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He worked as a plantation hand in Queensland. Aposenio was admitted to the Peel Island Lazaret in 1906 for mixed leprosy which housed non-Europeans, and died there in 1913.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives: Could not be located

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/184685812/aposenio​

entries.title: Alexander Appoo
entries.description:
Alexander Appoo

Sinhalese: ඇලෙක්‌සැන්ඩර් අප්පු 

 

Born: 1848,  Ceylon

Died:  1912,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Alexander Appoo was born in 1848 in Ceylon and came to Queensland onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He was admitted to Dunwich asylum and died there aged 64 years in 1912.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

 

Sources:

Bundaberg in print 110 years of news (2009)

​

entries.title: Matho Appo
entries.description:
Matho Appo

Sinhalese (possible): මැතෝ අප්පු 

 

Born: 1856, Ceylon

Died: 1942,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Matho Appo was born in 1856 in Ceylon and came to Mackay onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. A Matho Appo is listed in the local newspaper for being charged with arson for setting fire to a humpy in 1904. Appoo worked in Mackay for many years, and was admitted to Dunwich Asylum in 1942, where he died of cerebral thrombosis in 1942 at 86 years.

 

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/172462545?searchTerm=Matho%20Appo

2. https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/203441937/metho-appoo

​

entries.title: James Appo-Hame
entries.description:
James Appo-Hame

Sinhalese: ජේමිස් අප්පුහාමි 

 

Born: 1869,  Ceylon

Died: 1939,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

James Appo-Hame was born in 1869 in Ceylon and came to Queensland onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He earned a living cooking and gardening in various parts of Queensland, and was admitted to Dunwich Asylum in 1939, where he died aged 76 years of bronchial pneumonia.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/204255980/james-appoo-hame

​

entries.title: Uguay Pere
entries.description:
Uguay Pere

Sinhalese (possible): උක්කුවා පෙරේරා

 

Born: 1863,  Colombo, Ceylon

Died: 1937,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Uguay Pere was born in 1863 in Colombo and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He resided in Townsville and then admitted to Dunwich Asylum nine years after arrival due to his blindness. Pere stayed in Dunwich for 46 years, where he was well known as 'Blind Louis' and known for his ability to recall people's voices after a long time. He died in 1937 in Dunwich aged 75 years.

 

 

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/36880235?searchTerm=Uguay%20pere

2. https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/205042452/uguay-pere

entries.title: Sing Nah 'Seyab' Poo
entries.description:
'Seyab'/Sing Nah Poo

Sinhalese (possible): සින්නා අප්පු

 

Born: 1855,  Ceylon

Died: 1883,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Sing Nah 'Seyab' Poo was born in 1855 in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He worked as a labourer and was admitted to Dunwich Asylum in September 1883, nine months after arriving in Queensland. He died from dropsy in November 1883 aged only 28 years.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

entries.title: Jim Harmony
entries.description:
Jim Harmony

 

Born: 1861,  Ceylon

Died: 1946,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Jim Harmony was born in 1861 in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 to Bundaberg. He lived in Bundaberg as a labourer until he was admitted to Dunwich Benevolent Asylum in 1940 for being unable to take care of himself. He died in 1946 aged 85 years from myocardial degeneration.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia, Death Index, 1787-1985 [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2010.

entries.title: Charles Bundia
entries.description:
Charles Bundia

Sinhalese (possible): චාර්ලිස් බණ්ඩා

 

Born: 1839,  Ceylon

Died: 1922,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Charles Bundia was born in 1839 in Ceylon to Bunchu Rasa and Bumchu Manaka (most likely Punchi Menike) and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 to Bundaberg. He resided in Maryborough, Gympie and Bundaberg and last worked as a fruit grower and cook in Maryborough before he was admitted to Dunwich Benevolent Asylum, where he died in 1922 at the age of 83.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

entries.title: Mukhan Hassan
entries.description:
Mukhan Hassan

 

Born: 1856,  Ceylon

Died: 1948,  Sandgate, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Mukhan Hassan was born in 1856 in Ceylon and is one of the few non-Sinhalese among the 500 who came onboard the Devonshire in 1882. He worked as a labourer until he was admitted to Dunwich Benevolent Asylum in 1941 being unable to care for himself. He died in Sandgate in 1948 aged 92 years from concussion, bruises and senility.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

Original data: Find a Grave. Find a Grave. http://www.findagrave.com/cgi-bin/fg.cgi.

​

entries.title: Charles Deen
entries.description:
Charles Deen

 

Born: 1865, Ceylon

Died: 1913,  Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Charles Deen was born in 1865 in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. It is possible that he was also one of the few non-Sinhalese onboard the Devonshire, although in a local article his nationality is disputed by another Ceylonese migrant, Kallu (Call) Appoo. Deen was hanged for the murder of Peter Dina in 1913 in Boggs Road Gaol in Brisbane.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

Original data: Find a Grave. Find a Grave. http://www.findagrave.com/cgi-bin/fg.cgi.

​

entries.title: Sing Appu
entries.description:
Sing Appu

Possibly: සිඤ්ඤෝ අප්පු 

 

Born: Unknown,  Ceylon

Died: 1940,  Mackay, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Sing Appu was born in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He was employed at Homebush Sugar Mill in Mackay. It is possible that he was the Sing Appu who was termed the 'Homebush Murderer' for shooting an Arab named Mahomet Ali and fleeing into the bush. Sing Appu died in Mackay in 1940.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

Original data: Find a Grave. Find a Grave. http://www.findagrave.com/cgi-bin/fg.cgi.

entries.title: Abraham Weerarathna Jayasuria
entries.description:
Abraham Weerarathna Jayasuria

Sinhalese: අබරාන් වීරරත්න ජයසූරිය

 

Born: 1872,  Ceylon

Died: 1964,  Ingham, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Abraham Weerarathna Jayasuria was born in 1872 in Ceylon and it is likely that he came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 due to the limited methods of coming to Australia at the time. He was engaged in sugar growing, and resided for 45 years in Ingham district. He also experimented growing tea in Queensland. He died in 1964 in Ingham.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia, Marriage Index, 1788-1950 [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2010.

entries.title: Dinnie Silva
entries.description:
Dinnie Silva

 

Born: 1859,  Colombo, Ceylon

Died: 1945,  Dunwich, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Dinnie Silva was born in 1859 in Colombo and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882 aged 16 years. He worked as a cook and resided in Yeppoon, where he owned 20 acres of land, and also lived in Mackay and Homebush. Silva was admitted to Dunwich Benevolent Asylum in 1944 for being unable to take care of himself and died in 1945 of cerebral hemorrhage at the age of 86.

 

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia, Death Index, 1787-1985 [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2010.

entries.title: George W Gango
entries.description:
George W Gango

 

Born: 1855,  Ceylon

Died: 1947,  Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

George W Gango was born in 1855 in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He lived in Townsville, Ayr, Ingham and Innisfail and worked as a cook. Gango also owned a galvanised iron hut valued at 15 pounds. He was admitted to Dunwich Benevolent Asylum in 1939 and died in 1947 in Eventide, Sandgate of bronchial pneumonia and senility at the age of 93 years.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

entries.title: John James Wenitong
entries.description:
John James Wenitong

Sinhalese: ජෝන් ජේමිස් වනිගතුංග 

Born: 1872,  Ceylon

Died: 1946,  Gladstone, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

John James Wenitong was born in 1872 in Ceylon. He travelled onboard the Devonshire as a family with his parents Harmanis Wanigetunga and Lucy Hamey, and siblings David and Sarah, who later married Kallu (Call) Appoo. John married Eliza Ann Kathleen Wenitong before 1909, and electoral rolls show that John worked as a meatworker and resided in Gladstone. He died in 1946.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

Find A Grave 

 

Sources:

1. Ancestry.com. Australia and New Zealand, Find a Grave Index, 1800s-Current [database on-line]. Lehi, UT, USA: Ancestry.com Operations, Inc., 2012.

entries.title: Charlie Williams
entries.description:
Charlie Williams

 

Born: Unknown,  Ceylon

Died: 1893,  Appletree Creek, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Charlie Williams was born in Ceylon and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He was employed by cane farmer Mr C. Anderson in Appletree Creek, and died in 1893 from drowning.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

 

Sources:

Bundaberg in print 110 years of news (2009)

​

entries.title: Sing Apoo
entries.description:
Sing Apoo

Possibly: සිඤ්ඤෝ අප්පු 

 

Born: Unknown, Ceylon

Died: 1908,  Mackay, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Sing Apoo was born in Ceylon and came to Queensland onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He was held in a cell for the murder of an Arab man and committed suicide in the cell in 1908.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

 

Sources:

Bundaberg in print 110 years of news (2009)

​

entries.title: James Appoo
entries.description:
James Appoo

Sinhalese: ජේමිස් අප්පු

 

Born: 1869,  Unawatuna, Galle District, Ceylon (estimated)

Died: 1951, Cairns, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

James Appoo was born in Unawatuna, Galle District, Ceylon around 1869 and came onboard the Devonshire vessel in 1882 around age 13-14. Appoo was one of many migrants who disembarked at Mackay. His job is listed as "carrier" in 1914. An article seen below is written about him when he was about 90 years of age and a Cairns resident. It mentions that he later engaged in timber cutting and "hauling sleepers and firewood for sugar mills" which helped him to make a significant amount of income. Appoo died in Cairns in 1951. 
 

 

Records:

QLD Archives 

Find A Grave 

 

Sources:

1. https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/171345075

​

entries.title: Johannes
entries.description:
Johannes

 

Born: 1867,  Ceylon

Died: 1906,  Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

 

Description:

Johannes was born in Ceylon in 1867 and came onboard the SS Devonshire in 1882. He was charged with murder of a constable and hanged in Boggs Road Gaol in Brisbane in 1906.

 

Records:

QLD Archives

 

Sources:

Bundaberg in print 110 years of news (2009)

​
